evals.expected_output: Review identifying: SQL injection in raw query, hardcoded JWT secret, console.log leaking user data, no token expiry, passwords stored/compared in plaintext, no pagination on getAllUsers. Each issue explained clearly with before/after examples. Final summary with merge signal ⚠️.

assertions.description: Detects SQL injection vulnerability in raw query string
assertions.criteria: The review explicitly identifies the SQL injection risk in the raw template literal query and suggests using parameterized queries or an ORM
The review flags the hardcoded SECRET constant and recommends using environment variables (process.env)

assertions.description: Flags console.log leaking sensitive user data
assertions.criteria: The review identifies that console.log('User logged in:', user) leaks sensitive user data and recommends removing it or logging only non-sensitive identifiers

assertions.description: Emits ⚠️ Fix before opening merge signal
assertions.criteria: The final summary includes a clear signal that the PR should NOT be opened yet (⚠️ or equivalent warning)

assertions.description: Includes plain-language explanation for junior devs
assertions.criteria: At least one finding includes a plain-language explanation of why the issue matters in practice, suitable for a developer with 6 months of experience

evals.expected_output: Review identifying: handleDelete is a wrapper that adds no value (unnecessary indirection), array.sort() mutates the original users array (should use [...users].sort()), missing useCallback on handleDelete. Overall code is clean so score should be decent. Merge signal could be ✅ or ⚠️ depending on severity assessment.

assertions.description: Detects that .sort() mutates the original array
assertions.criteria: The review identifies that users.sort() mutates the original array prop and recommends spreading first ([...users].sort()) or using toSorted()

assertions.description: Flags handleDelete as an unnecessary wrapper
assertions.criteria: The review points out that handleDelete is a thin wrapper around onDelete that adds no logic, and suggests either removing it or noting it would make sense with useCallback
The review includes at least one before/after code example illustrating how to fix a finding

assertions.description: Scores all three dimensions (best practices, performance, security)
assertions.criteria: The review includes numeric scores (X/10) for all three dimensions: best practices/readability, performance/optimization, and security/bugs

evals.expected_output: Review identifying: use of 'any' type for data params (TypeScript best practice), no error handling on any API call, localStorage in a service layer (should be abstracted), hardcoded base URL (should use env var or config), unvalidated 'category' and 'id' params going directly into URLs. Merge signal ⚠️.

assertions.criteria: The review flags the use of 'any' as the type for the data parameter and recommends defining proper TypeScript interfaces or types
The review notes the absence of try/catch or error handling on the async API calls

assertions.description: Flags localStorage access inside a service layer
assertions.criteria: The review identifies that accessing localStorage directly in a service layer is a bad practice and recommends abstracting token access or passing it as a parameter
The final summary includes an overall PR score or rating
The final summary includes a prioritized list of at least 3 critical issues to fix before merging
